body: PT Hasnur Internasional Shipping Tbk (Hais) targets a 5% revenue increase and 4% net profit growth by 2025, driven by the addition of new vessels to meet growing customer demand, with plans to add four sets of tugboats and barges to its fleet. In the January-September 2024 period, the company recorded IDR 765 billion in revenue, up from IDR 681 billion in the same period in 2023, despite a drop in gross profit and net profit.
title: PT Hais Targets 5% Revenue and 4% Net Profit Growth by 2025 with Fleet Expansion.

body: Hardi Wijaya Liong acquired 837.58 million shares of PT Provident Investasi Bersama Tbk, holding 5.31% of the company's shares, while Winato Kartono increased his ownership in the company to 7.77% by purchasing 252.71 million shares. Other notable transactions include PT HP Capital Resources acquiring 378.55 million shares of PT Trisula Textile Industries Tbk, CGS International Securities Singapore Pte Ltd acquiring 153 million shares of PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk, and DBS Bank Ltd S/A Pemberton Asian Opportunities Fund purchasing 10 million shares of PT Sarimelati Kencana Tbk.
title: Multiple investors acquire significant stakes in various Indonesian companies across different sectors.

body: PT Indofood CBP Sukses Makmur Tbk (INDF) clarified that four Indomie products were withdrawn from the Australian market due to parallel imports by non-official distributors, not due to non-compliance with regulations. The company confirmed that its officially exported instant noodle products to Australia continue to be marketed and distributed normally, with no potential sanctions from Australian authorities, and that all products comply with Indonesian and international standards.
title: Indofood clarifies withdrawal of four Indomie products from Australian market, citing non-official parallel imports.

body: PT Trans Bahtera Pioneer (TBP) will invest IDR 200 billion to purchase six sets of tugboats and barges, aiming to reduce reliance on external logistics providers and optimize transportation costs for coal supply. The joint venture between PT Resources Alam Indonesia TBK (TKLD) and PT Trans Power Marine TBK (TPMA) is expected to create long-term added value, enhance profitability, and strengthen their competitive position in the coal logistics industry.
title: PT Trans Bahtera Pioneer to Invest IDR 200 Billion in Six Tugboats and Barges Following Joint Venture Agreement.

body: Modalku Group, a peer-to-peer lending platform, has secured a USD 25 million investment from Cool Japan Fund, equivalent to IDR 398 billion, to drive growth in Southeast Asia's digital financial services sector. The investment follows significant milestones achieved by Modalku Group in 2024, including strategic equity investments from Maybank and a third annual credit facility from HSBC's ASEAN Growth Fund, with cumulative commitments exceeding IDR 1.59 quadrillion in credit facilities.
title: Modalku Group Secures USD 25 Million Investment from Cool Japan Fund to Boost Digital Financial Services in Southeast Asia.

body: PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BRI) assured the public that its customer data and funds remain secure, with all banking systems and transaction services operating normally, following rumors of a potential data breach. The bank's director of digital information technology clarified that customers can continue using all BRI services, including digital banking platforms, without any security concerns.
title: Bank Rakyat Indonesia assures customers that their data and funds are secure following rumors of a potential data breach.

body: CGS International Securities Singapore Pte Ltd increased its stake in PT Astrindo Nusantara Infrastruktur Tbk (BIPi) to nearly 16% by acquiring 155 million shares, while Sendra Gunawan raised her ownership stake in PT Asuransi Harta Aman Pratama Tbk to 12.56% by purchasing 112.5 million shares. Meanwhile, foreign investors such as HSBC-Fund Svs A/C Chengdong Investment Corp-Self and Inv Management Pte Ltd sold significant stakes in PT Bumi Resources Tbk and PT ITSec Asia Tbk, respectively.
title: Foreign and local investors make notable transactions in Indonesian stocks, shifting ownership stakes in various companies.

body: Honour Accord Limited is set to acquire 80% of PT Aesler Grup International Tbk (RONY) from its two major shareholders, PT Nakula Investama Indonesia and PT Karimun Jawa Pratama, by taking over 1 billion shares. The deal, if finalized, would make Honour Accord the controlling shareholder, and RONY's stock price has surged 513.17% to IDR 296 per share, with a 20% free float representing 250 million shares held by 196 non-controlling shareholders.
title: Honour Accord Limited to Acquire 80% Stake in PT Aesler Grup International from Major Shareholders.

body: PT Sinergi Inti Andalan Prima Tbk (INET) projects a 20% revenue increase to IDR 42.02 billion in 2025, driven by growing corporate entity customers, following a 50.42% revenue surge to IDR 35.02 billion in 2024 from IDR 23.81 billion in 2023. The company also aims for a 194.51% net profit jump to IDR 2.57 billion in the fourth quarter of this year, with its shares fluctuating between IDR 62 and IDR 65 per share in early trading.
title: PT Sinergi Inti Andalan Prima TBK projects 20% revenue increase to IDR 42.02 billion in 2025.

body: Bank Indonesia's decision to keep interest rates unchanged at 6% is expected to lead to a steady offloading trend in Indonesia's stock market, causing investors to adopt a "wait and see" approach due to perceived economic instability. The Jakarta Composite Index has declined for five consecutive trading days, closing at 7,107.88 on Wednesday, down 0.70% from the previous day, as foreign investors are more likely to park their funds elsewhere rather than invest in Indonesia's stock market.
title: Bank Indonesia's decision to keep interest rates unchanged may lead to a steady offloading trend in the country's stock market.

body: PT Murni Sadar Tbk (MTMH) has acquired 80% shares of PT Global Genetika Indonesia for IDR 3.56 billion to enhance its medical laboratory services, with the health examination segment contributing 23% to its total revenue of IDR 807.29 billion in Q3 2024. The acquisition includes all shares, assets, and receivables of PT Global Genetika Indonesia, which operates in the clinical laboratory sector, and is expected to strengthen MTMH's position in the healthcare industry.
title: Murni Teguh Hospitals acquires 80% shares of PT Global Genetika Indonesia to enhance medical laboratory services.
